title: Sleeping Gods - The Spoiler-Filled Look
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eLcRSVAncfc

Daniel (@dungeondive) opens the hood on Sleeping Gods after completing a campaign — spoilers throughout. He finished with a score of 65 and ending number 12 of 13, having found just 4 of the game's 75 totems and solved 15 quests, leaving a staggering amount undiscovered (he fans out the huge stacks of quests, adventure cards and totems he never even saw).

His chief disappointment is what carries between campaigns: essentially only your notes and your growing player knowledge, plus a couple of unlocked quests. He wishes the game leaned harder into roguelike carryover — the small "carrot on the stick" left him a little deflated rather than eager to dive straight back in. He counsels taking meticulous notes, and gently criticises some quest clues as so vague they send you wandering the wrong end of the map (the infamous "city surrounded by thunderstorms" hunt). The dungeons he found punishing and unintuitive enough that he may simply skip them.

But the affection wins out: a character, Laurent, actually died and went back in the box, and Daniel got emotional about it. Combat scaling is erratic and the world deliberately doesn't scale by location — a bold choice he applauds. Still a treasured top-ten adventure game.

----

In a campaign game, how much carryover between playthroughs do you want — a clean reset each time, or a roguelike snowball of permanent gains?
